name: gridsource

metadata.summary: Data mining with XLSX, cfg, json, etc.

documentation:
==========
GridSource
==========


Data management with XLSX, cfg, json, etc.


* Free software: MIT license


Features
--------

* TODO

functions.name: gridsource.utils.safe_isnan
documentation:
Make np.isnan working on any type:

```pycon
>>> test = {False: False, "": False, None: True, 1: False, np.nan:True}
>>> for test, exp in test.items():
...     assert safe_isnan(test) == exp
```

None can be checked as `np.NaN` depending on `extend_to_None`:

```pycon
>>> safe_isnan(None, extend_to_None=False)
False
>>> safe_isnan(None, extend_to_None=True)  # default
True
```

functions.name: gridsource.utils.fmt_dict
documentation:
```pycon
>>> fmt_dict(attributes=["mandatory"])
{'bold': True, 'font_color': '#3A81CC', 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'border': 1, 'bg_color': '#fcaf3e', 'color': 'black'}
```

modules.name: gridsource.autodoc
documentation: Autodoc to create automatic documentation

functions.name: gridsource.autodoc._write
documentation: a few RST helpers

functions.name: gridsource.autodoc._indent
documentation:
---
param indent: true
param txt: true
return: true
---

functions.name: gridsource.autodoc._directive
documentation:
---
param arg: the argument to pass into the directive
param content: the text to write into this element
param fields: fields to append as children underneath the directive
param indent: (optional default=0) number of characters to indent this element
param name: the directive itself to use
return: true
---

Add a comment with a not-named directive:

```pycon
>>> print(_directive(content='bla\nbla'))
.. bla
   bla
```

functions.name: gridsource.autodoc._new_public_file
documentation:
create a file at `filename` path structured as follows:

titles[0][0], leveled at level titlels[0][1]
titles[1][0], leveled at level titlels[2][1]
...

Please edit {filename} to provide adequate description.

includes[0]

includes[1]

...

modules.name: gridsource.validation
documentation: Validator module.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: gridsource.validation.yamlcat
documentation:
concat YAML files and returns a StringIO object

files is an iterable of possibly:
: - StringIO object
  - strings:
    \- YAML content (shall begin with "---")
    \- path (str) to an existing valid YAML file
    \- path (Path obj) to an existing valid YAML file

```pycon
>>> f1 = StringIO('''
... ---
... _length: &length
...   units: mm
...   default: 0
... key1: 5
... ''')
>>> f2 = '''
... ---
... key2: 15
... key3:
...   <<: *length
... '''
>>> print(yamlcat(f1, f2).getvalue())
---
_length: &length
  units: mm
  default: 0
key1: 5
key2: 15
key3:
  <<: *length
```

functions.name: gridsource.validation.load_yaml
documentation:
concatenate and load yaml files
Wrapper for:
\* yamlcat(files)
\* yaml.load()

if `debug` is True, return a tuple:

: (YAML dictionnary translation, YAML concatenated file)

otherwise, return the YAML dictionnary translation

```pycon
>>> f1 = StringIO('''
... ---
... _length: &length
...   units: mm
...   default: 0
... key1: 5
... ''')
>>> f2 = '''
... ---
... key2: 15
... key3:
...   <<: *length
... key4:
...   <<: *length
...   default: 1
... '''
>>> load_yaml(f1, f2) == {'key1': 5,
...                       'key2': 15,
...                       'key3': {'units': 'mm', 'default': 0},
...                       'key4': {'units': 'mm', 'default': 1}}
True
```

methods.name: gridsource.validation.DataFrameSchematizer.validate_dataframe
documentation:
validate dataframe against self.schema():

1. type validation
2. units conversion
3. default values are applied
4. final validation

utility class to build a schema (jsonschema) for a Pandas DataFrame

Given a DataFrame like:

```pycon
>>> df = pd.DataFrame({ "id": {7: 0, 1: 1, 2:5},
...                    "name": {7: "Doe", 1: "Fante", 2: "Mercury"},
...                    "firstname": {7: "John", 2: "Freddy", 1:"Richard"},
...                    "age": {7: '42', 1: 22},
...                    "life_nb": {7: 5, 1: 'hg', 2: 15}})
```

We can build a column-wise schema:

```pycon
>>> v = DataFrameSchematizer()
>>> v.add_column(name='id', types='integer', unique=True, mandatory=True)
>>> v.add_column(name='name', types='string', mandatory=True)
>>> v.add_column(name='firstname', types='string')
>>> v.add_column(name='age', types='integer', mandatory=False, default=0)
>>> v.add_column(name='life_nb', types='integer', mandatory=True, maximum=4)
>>> v._is_units  # no units declared in any column
False
```

And validate the DataFrame:

```pycon
>>> df, is_valid, errors = v.validate_dataframe(df)
>>> pprint(errors)
{('age', 0): ["'42' is not valid under any of the given schemas",
              "'42' is not of type 'integer'",
              "'42' is not of type 'null'"],
 ('life_nb', 0): ['5 is greater than the maximum of 4'],
 ('life_nb', 1): ["'hg' is not of type 'integer'"],
 ('life_nb', 2): ['15 is greater than the maximum of 4']}
```

validate_dataframe returned a namedtuple:

```pycon
>>> val = v.validate_dataframe(df)
>>> val._fields
('df', 'isok', 'report')
>>> val.isok
False
```

The schema used for validation can be accessed by:

```pycon
>>> schema = v.build()
>>> pprint(schema)
{'$schema': 'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#',
 'properties': {'age': {'items': {'anyOf': [{'type': 'integer'},
                                            {'type': 'null'}],
                                  'default': 0},
                        'type': 'array',
                        'uniqueItems': False},
                'firstname': {'items': {'anyOf': [{'type': 'string'},
                                                  {'type': 'null'}]},
                              'type': 'array',
                              'uniqueItems': False},
                'id': {'items': {'type': 'integer'},
                       'type': 'array',
                       'uniqueItems': True},
                'life_nb': {'items': {'maximum': 4, 'type': 'integer'},
                            'type': 'array',
                            'uniqueItems': False},
                'name': {'items': {'type': 'string'},
                         'type': 'array',
                         'uniqueItems': False}},
 'required': ['id', 'name', 'life_nb'],
 'type': 'object'}
```

We can also build a basic schema and populate `DataFrameSchematizer` with it:

```pycon
>>> schema = {
...           'id': {'types': 'integer', 'unique': True, 'mandatory': True},
...           'name': {'types': 'string', 'mandatory': True},
...           'firstname': {'types': 'string'},
...           'age': {'types': 'integer', 'minimum': 0, 'default':0},
...           'life_nb': {'types': 'integer', 'mandatory': True, 'maximum': 4}
...           }
```

```pycon
>>> v = DataFrameSchematizer()
>>> v.add_columns(schema)
```

Or via a JSON string

```pycon
>>> schema = (
...   '{"id": {"types": "integer", "unique": true, "mandatory": true}, "name": '
...   '{"types": "string", "mandatory": true}, "firstname": {"types": "string"}, '
...   '"age": {"types": "integer", "minimum": 0, "default": 0}, "life_nb": {"types": "integer", '
...   '"mandatory": true, "maximum": 4}}')
>>> v.add_columns(schema)
>>> df, is_valid, errors = v.validate_dataframe(df)
>>> pprint(errors)
{('age', 0): ["'42' is not valid under any of the given schemas",
              "'42' is not of type 'integer'",
              "'42' is not of type 'null'"],
 ('life_nb', 0): ['5 is greater than the maximum of 4'],
 ('life_nb', 1): ["'hg' is not of type 'integer'"],
 ('life_nb', 2): ['15 is greater than the maximum of 4']}
```

Or via a YAML string

```pycon
>>> schema = '''
... ---
... id:
...   types: integer
...   unique: true
...   mandatory: true
... name:
...   types: string
...   mandatory: true
... firstname:
...   types: string
... age:
...   types: integer
...   minimum: 0
...   default: 0
... life_nb:
...   types: integer
...   mandatory: true
...   maximum: 4
... '''
>>> v.add_columns(schema)
```

And validate the DataFrame:

```pycon
>>> df, is_valid, errors = v.validate_dataframe(df)
>>> pprint(errors)
{('age', 0): ["'42' is not valid under any of the given schemas",
              "'42' is not of type 'integer'",
              "'42' is not of type 'null'"],
 ('life_nb', 0): ['5 is greater than the maximum of 4'],
 ('life_nb', 1): ["'hg' is not of type 'integer'"],
 ('life_nb', 2): ['15 is greater than the maximum of 4']}
```

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.get_all_units
documentation: return a dictionnary of default units

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.getunits
documentation:
retrieve from schema the working units. Raise KeyError the the
column is not units-aware.

if `key` is provided, the relevant units is returned (eg. "length" -> "mm")
otherwise, the whole dict is returned

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.getrow
documentation: retrieve a row from any tabname, eventually sorting by `search_by`

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.getvalue
documentation:
retrive a cell content by intersecting row/column index

if as_quantity is:
  * `True`: return a quantity argument *if possible*
  * `False`: return magnitude

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.convert_to
documentation:
convert units-aware dataframe to units
units can be:
* None: will convert to base units
* string: eg. 'm^3'
* a dict mapping columns to units

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin._set_schema
documentation: assign a schema to a tab

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.read_schema
documentation: assign a global schema by parsing the given filepath

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin._validate_tab
documentation: validate a tab using the provided scheme

methods.name: gridsource.validation.ValidatorMixin.validate
documentation:
iterate through all tabs and validate eachone

`row_reporting_offset` can be used to offset rows reporting
`readonly` is mainly usefull for testing purpose, to chain validation
